title: On the naming of the series
display_title: On the naming of the series
wikitext:
[]: On the naming of the series             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Jan 19, 2009 5:44 pm So Neon Genesis means new beginning, right? Does that mean that the title is a trippy description of Unit 1? After all, Unit 1 was kind of the thing that set off 3I, meaning that it's the Evangelion responsible for a new beginning. Seems like a logical jump to say Unit 1 == the Neon Genesis Evangelion, right? Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Mon Jan 19, 2009 7:26 pm I always figured it referred to the idea of the series as a whole, in that by the end a new beginning for mankind was established. I would consider Unit 01 the catalyst, however.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:03 pm It's been long established that the name only stands as "sounding cool." That is all.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:14 pm Well, he wasn't referring to the name of the show, but in choosing the word "Evangelion" Anno Talks to Kids wrote: Student: Why is that robot-looking thing called Evangelion? Anno: It's a Christian word meaning, "Fukuin", or "Gospel" and it's supposed to bring blessings. Actually, it's a Greek word. I used it because it sounds complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:32 pm Ornette wrote: Well, he wasn't referring to the name of the show, but in choosing the word "Evangelion" Anno Talks to Kids wrote: Student: Why is that robot-looking thing called Evangelion? Anno: It's a Christian word meaning, "Fukuin", or "Gospel" and it's supposed to bring blessings. Actually, it's a Greek word. I used it because it sounds complicated.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 19, 2009 9:08 pm Rather convenient how "Evangelion" shortens to "Eva". I wonder if they used that as a starting point for constructing the show's "mythology", AFTER chosing a title and a name for the "robots". Sadamoto: "Hey, Hideaki, let's call the show 'Evangelion'. It's a lot cooler than that other title you've been pitching." Anno : "Well... I'll consider it." Sadamoto: "It's Greek. It means 'gospel' or some crap." Masayuki: "That'd be a cool name for the main robot, too. 'Evangelion'. Really rolls off the tongue." Tsurumaki: "You can shorten it to 'Eva'. You know, like the first woman in that Bible mythology." Sadamoto: "Exactly! We can use Western religion to establish the main elements for the show." Tsurumaki: "That sounds awesome. Western religion is so fucked up. They're always nailing people to trees." Anno : "We'll need to have a lot of crucifictions, then. More than any anime currently in existence." Tsurumaki: "Crucifiction... Cross... How about the bad guys use crosses as a weapon? Wait... no ! They can make huge explosions that TURN INTO crosses!" Yamashita: "And there should be this weapon, like the one they stabbed Jesus with, but REALLY big!" Anno : "So if our robots are 'Eva', I guess that means they have to come from an Adam." Sadamoto : "Maybe the Evas aren't really robots at all!" Yamashita : "Giant warrior women?" Masasyuki : "Nice. Then we can give them humungous knockers. That would make animating them a lot more fun." Anno : "No, no, no. Then the audience would expect them to be launchable as missiles, and it would take all the menace away." Yamashita: "Erm, alright... How about... armored giants... artificial humanoids... copied from 'Adam', who was, in turn, a creation of the Atlanteans?" Anno : "Tch, Ikuto... We can't do the thing with Adam and Atlantis again ." Tsurumaki: "So change the name. Instead of 'Atlanteans', make it... 'The Progenitors', or something." Etc., etc.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Jan 19, 2009 10:50 pm Reichu's post is pure win. I'd never really tried to analyze the title, but what the original poster says makes sense. Of course, it's a well know fact that if you make a piece of art vague enough people will make their own conclusions and they'll make them sound damn convincing. The people will follow. We call this 'literary criticism'. Still, regardless of intention, I've always loved that as a biblical pun. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jan 20, 2009 12:07 am Reichu: WIN! But yeah, it was to sound cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jan 20, 2009 10:17 am Reichu wrote: Rather convenient how "Evangelion" shortens to "Eva". I wonder if they used that as a starting point for constructing the show's "mythology", AFTER chosing a title and a name for the "robots". Sadamoto: "Hey, Hideaki, let's call the show 'Evangelion'. It's a lot cooler than that other title you've been pitching." Anno : "Well... I'll consider it." Sadamoto: "It's Greek. It means 'gospel' or some crap." Masayuki: "That'd be a cool name for the main robot, too. 'Evangelion'. Really rolls off the tongue." Tsurumaki: "You can shorten it to 'Eva'. You know, like the first woman in that Bible mythology." Sadamoto: "Exactly! We can use Western religion to establish the main elements for the show." Tsurumaki: "That sounds awesome. Western religion is so fucked up. They're always nailing people to trees." Anno : "We'll need to have a lot of crucifictions, then. More than any anime currently in existence." Tsurumaki: "Crucifiction... Cross... How about the bad guys use crosses as a weapon? Wait... no ! They can make huge explosions that TURN INTO crosses!" Yamashita: "And there should be this weapon, like the one they stabbed Jesus with, but REALLY big!" Anno : "So if our robots are 'Eva', I guess that means they have to come from an Adam." Sadamoto : "Maybe the Evas aren't really robots at all!" Yamashita : "Giant warrior women?" Masasyuki : "Nice. Then we can give them humungous knockers. That would make animating them a lot more fun." Anno : "No, no, no. Then the audience would expect them to be launchable as missiles, and it would take all the menace away." Yamashita: "Erm, alright... How about... armored giants... artificial humanoids... copied from 'Adam', who was, in turn, a creation of the Atlanteans?" Anno : "Tch, Ikuto... We can't do the thing with Adam and Atlantis again ." Tsurumaki: "So change the name. Instead of 'Atlanteans', make it... 'The Progenitors', or something." Etc., etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 20, 2009 5:03 pm Reichu: Is it sad that I could imagine that conversation taking place exactly in that way? Anyway, the title itself is meant to sound cool, but in some ways could be a reference to 3rd Impact or the state of the world afterwards since it's a "new beginning" in that sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Jan 21, 2009 9:39 am One question: What if that *was* how they put the series together?
